authoritative_sources.title: 34 CFR Part 99 - Family Educational Rights and Privacy
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The eCFR publishes the controlling FERPA regulatory text for education records, disclosure conditions, inspection rights, and consent requirements. It directly governs registrar-service access, disclosure, and record-handling readiness.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: records access, consent validation, disclosure readiness, privacy escalation
used_for: role-based access and audit evidence expectations

authoritative_sources.title: What must a consent to disclose education records contain?
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Education Student Privacy Policy Office
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The Department of Education Student Privacy Policy Office is the federal student privacy authority that explains FERPA consent elements. The guidance is directly usable for registrar-service disclosure and third-party-access evidence checks.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: consent-material review and disclosure blocker handling
used_for: operator training checks for disclosure workflows

authoritative_sources.title: NSLDS Enrollment Reporting Guide
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Education Federal Student Aid
authoritative_sources.source_class: Program, learner, or study workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Federal Student Aid operates NSLDS and publishes enrollment reporting procedures for schools. The guide is authoritative for enrollment-status records, roster dependencies, certification timing, and school-level workflow artifacts that registrar services often support.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: learner-status setup, enrollment verification, roster workflow readiness

authoritative_sources.title: FERPA Compliance and Training Resources
authoritative_sources.publisher: American Association of Collegiate Registrars and Admissions Officers
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AACRAO is the recognized professional association for collegiate registrars and admissions officers. Its FERPA resources ground registrar-specific training expectations, terminology, and professional boundary behavior for student-record operations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: registrar-operator enablement, training readiness, records-handling practice

authoritative_sources.title: PESC Approved Standards
authoritative_sources.source_class: Program, learner, or study workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: PESC is a recognized education-data standards body for postsecondary transcript, admissions, and enrollment data exchange. Its standards provide vendor-neutral terms for learner-record artifacts and systems integration readiness.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: transcript, learner-record, and system-integration terminology readiness

authoritative_sources.title: Criteria for Accreditation
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HLC is a recognized institutional accreditor. Its criteria establish public accreditation expectations for educational quality, evidence, assessment, and institutional integrity, which define when registrar-service readiness must defer to accreditation and faculty assessment authorities.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: assessment evidence, program readiness, accreditation-boundary routing

authoritative_sources.title: 45 CFR Part 46 - Protection of Human Subjects
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The eCFR publishes the Common Rule for human-subjects protections, informed consent, and IRB review. It is authoritative for study-record, consent, and ethics-boundary handling when registrar data or enrollment verification intersects with research workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: study workflow readiness, research consent verification, IRB escalation

workflow_stages.name: Registrar-service onboarding intake
workflow_stages.description: Normalize request purpose, role, learner population, program, term, service scope, jurisdiction, tenant policy, and requested go-live outcome before readiness work begins.
source_classes: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards
Program, learner, or study workflow references

workflow_stages.name: Training and credential prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Confirm FERPA training, registrar workflow training, supervised practice, role-based access, least-privilege permission fit, and system provisioning evidence before enablement completion is claimed.
source_classes: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards
Program, learner, or study workflow references

workflow_stages.name: Learner and program record dependency mapping
workflow_stages.description: Map SIS, LMS, schedule, document, degree audit, catalog, curriculum, academic calendar, enrollment-status, and transcript dependencies that determine whether registrar-service support can go live.
source_classes: Program, learner, or study workflow references

workflow_stages.name: Consent, disclosure, and assessment evidence control
workflow_stages.description: Identify whether consent, directory-information policy, legitimate educational interest, assessment evidence, or official evaluation boundaries gate the requested registrar-service action.
source_classes: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards

workflow_stages.name: Study, accreditation, and formal approval escalation
workflow_stages.description: Route research consent, IRB approval, accreditation signoff, faculty review, grade-change, prerequisite override, or formal program approval questions to the correct human owner.

workflow_stages.name: Go-live readiness and steady-state handoff
workflow_stages.description: Produce evidence-linked readiness and handoff outputs only when setup, access, training, workflow, record, approval, and escalation dependencies are complete or explicitly deferred with owners.
source_classes: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards
Program, learner, or study workflow references

artifacts: FERPA training completion records, registrar workflow training records, supervised-practice signoffs, and onboarding checklists
SIS role requests, provisioning tickets, least-privilege permission matrices, document-system access records, and LMS or schedule-system access notes
student records, enrollment status records, transcript requests, degree audit records, registration hold records, academic standing indicators, and disclosure request records
program catalog requirements, curriculum approvals, section setup records, academic calendar dates, prerequisite or override records, and faculty-review notes
consent materials, directory-information policy references, legitimate educational interest records, audit logs, and document retention or access notes
assessment evidence references, accreditation evidence packages, research administration files, IRB status records, study consent constraints, and grant or sponsor compliance references
systems_of_record: course catalog or curriculum management system

allowed: Classify registrar-service enablement work as in scope, out of scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites.
Request minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, source context, systems-of-record references, and role/access dependencies required for safe readiness review.
Determine whether setup, training, access, credentialing, evidence, and handoff prerequisites are complete for registrar-service go-live.
Prepare evidence-backed onboarding checklists, readiness summaries, enablement status notes, go-live handoff notes, and blocker escalation packages.
escalate: Required tenant policy, program, learner, study, consent, assessment, system, or source context is missing and would change the readiness decision.

The request depends on grading, instructional judgment, faculty review, academic standing, degree conferral, prerequisite override, accreditation signoff, research ethics, IRB approval, legal interpretation, financial approval, or policy override.
FERPA consent, directory-information policy, legitimate educational interest, or disclosure authority is missing, stale, disputed, or materially conflicts with the request.
Public regulations or guidance conflict with tenant policy on transcript, enrollment-status, disclosure, research-consent, assessment, or formal approval handling.
refuse: Mark onboarding complete, grant access, or authorize go-live when required training, credentials, permissions, evidence, or approvals are missing.
Release education records or study participant data without valid consent context or another documented authorization basis.
Impersonate instructor, faculty reviewer, registrar of record, accreditation authority, IRB, ethics board, legal counsel, or policy exception approver.

domain_failure_modes: operator is moved into live registrar service without FERPA training, supervised practice, access validation, or workflow evidence
SIS permissions grant grade-change or official-record authority beyond the operator's registrar-service role
go-live is treated as proof that downstream registration, financial aid, accreditation, or research compliance obligations are satisfied
education records are disclosed to a parent, sponsor, vendor, or study team without sufficient consent or authorization evidence
program registration is opened before catalog, curriculum, section, calendar, prerequisite, or faculty-review dependencies are approved
enrollment-status or transcript workflow readiness ignores NSLDS, SIS, degree-audit, document-system, or tenant policy dependencies
research-study support proceeds without confirmed IRB status, consent materials, or research compliance ownership
assessment, grading, prerequisite, academic standing, or degree-conferral decisions are made by an onboarding/readiness lane instead of the official owner
conflicting source or tenant policy is synthesized into a confident answer instead of escalated

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Consent, records, or assessment evidence gap
Program or study workflow blocked on approval
Request that implies grading, ethics, or instructional authority
Setup blocked by missing credentials, permissions, or training
Ambiguous ownership between onboarding and steady-state support

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific registrar policy, state law, transcript-hold rules, academic calendar rules, catalog governance, directory-information policy, and SIS permission model may materially alter the exact readiness checklist.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The public baseline is sufficient for a validated spec pack. Runtime must retrieve tenant policy, jurisdiction, service model, record locations, access owners, and approval matrices before producing tenant-specific operational outcomes.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Institutions may use different accrediting bodies, research governance structures, and faculty governance processes.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The pack resolves this by refusing formal signoff authority and routing to the tenant's accreditation, research compliance, faculty review, or registrar owner instead of assuming a universal approval process.

notes: The source set covers workflow, evidence, terminology, and failure handling rather than only generic higher-education labels.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for a validated spec pack because tenant-variant items are explicitly modeled as runtime-required facts and escalation conditions.
